Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1: Search Linear for existing tickets related to NorthWind relocations or chain-of-custody tracking using linear_list_issues (query: "NorthWind" or "chain of custody" or "custody" or similar, team: "team_engineering"). Find NorthWind-related relocation tracking issues on the engineering and operations teams, including tickets about Raj Patel's server lab move and documentation gaps that Chloe identified during her ops reconstruction.
OE 2: Read comments on found NorthWind Linear issues using linear_list_comments to understand the full discussion history find details about the chain-of-custody protocol breach (movers arrived 2 hours early, loaded without the required IT security escort, servers sat overnight in a warehouse), Chloe's ops timeline reconstruction, and any engineering-specific follow-ups.
OE 3: Search Slack for NorthWind and chain-of-custody discussions using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "NorthWind" or "chain of custody" or "Raj Patel" or "Victor Huang" or "server relocation" or similar). Find Chloe's reconstruction in #operations (C006) documenting that the Linear ticket was closed without a custody log and Airtable shows "In Progress" despite the move being completed. Find David Chen's escalation messages in #customer-engagement (C002) about NorthWind being in save-or-lose mode and needing a coordinated retention response by EOD Friday.
OE 4: Search emails for the NorthWind chain-of-custody breach using search_emails (query: "Victor Huang" or "chain of custody" or "NorthWind" or "security breach" or similar). Find Victor Huang's (NorthWind CISO) furious breach report detailing the protocol violation on Raj Patel's server lab relocation. Find Pam Kowalski's final warning email to David Chen naming both the chain-of-custody breach and Emilia Cruz's piano damage, mentioning PathFinder Logistics as a competitor they've been talking to, and requesting a retention proposal by Friday.
OE 5: Search Airtable for the Raj Patel relocation record using airtable_search_records (query: "Raj Patel" or "NorthWind" or similar) to see what was actually documented in the system. Find the relocation record showing the server lab move details and special requirements (4 rack-mounted servers, ~2,400 lbs, lithium UPS batteries, chain-of-custody requirements from NorthWind CISO) but note the absence of any custody handoff logging fields the system only tracks status, coordinator, origin/destination, and special requirements text, with no structured fields for signed handoffs or audit timestamps.
OE 6: Search Airtable for other relocation records involving sensitive or high-value equipment using airtable_search_records (query: "prototype" or "lab" or "medical" or "sensitive" or "cryogenic" or "laser" or similar). Find multiple affected cases including: Dr. Anand Mehta / Canopy Health (2x Eizo calibrated diagnostic monitors at ~$12K each plus 350-lb secured filing cabinet requiring chain-of-custody documentation), Zara Okoye / Mosaic Robotics (2 robotic arm prototypes at $180K insured requiring anti-vibration foam crating), Keiko Tanaka / Mosaic Robotics (400-lb industrial laser cutter, Class 3B, DOT hazmat), Mei-Lin Zhao / GreenStack Energy (Class 9 lithium-ion battery test modules), Priya Venkatesh / GreenStack Energy (oscilloscope, PCB testing rig), and Emilia Cruz / NorthWind (Steinway grand piano). These cases demonstrate a pattern of high-value and sensitive-item moves that would all benefit from formal custody tracking.
OE 7: Look up Samira Tariq's contact details using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Samira Tariq" or "Samira" or similar). Find samira.tariq@moveops.com to use as the recipient for the email.
OE 8: Create a new Linear issue for the chain-of-custody tracking system limitation using linear_create_issue (team: "team_engineering", title: describing the custody handoff logging gap, description: documenting the system limitation no signed handoff fields, no audit timestamps, no tamper-evidence tracking with the NorthWind Raj Patel breach as the triggering case and the other affected sensitive-equipment moves as evidence of the pattern, priority: 2). OR if a relevant existing ticket is found in OE 1, add a comment using linear_create_comment (issueId: the found issue ID, body: documenting the custody tracking gap findings, the NorthWind case details, and the list of other affected sensitive-equipment moves).
OE 9: Post a summary message in #engineering (channel C003) using conversations_add_message (channel_id: "C003", payload: summary of the chain-of-custody tracking gap the system doesn't support custody handoff logging, the NorthWind CISO breach report exposed the limitation, and multiple other sensitive-equipment moves share the same gap. Note that David is assembling a retention response and engineering needs to be aware of what can and cannot be committed).
OE 10: Send an email from dmitri.volkov@moveops.com to samira.tariq@moveops.com using send_email (sender: "dmitri.volkov@moveops.com", recipients: ["samira.tariq@moveops.com"]). Content should cover: the technical gap in the tracking system (no custody handoff logging, no signed handoffs, no audit timestamps), the NorthWind Raj Patel case as the triggering incident, the scope of other affected sensitive-equipment moves discovered across Airtable, and a recommendation that Samira prioritize scoping this capability given the NorthWind account is in save-or-lose territory and David is preparing a retention proposal.

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

OE 1: Search Linear for existing tickets related to NorthWind relocations or chain-of-custody tracking using linear_list_issues (query: "NorthWind" or "chain of custody" or "custody" or similar, team: "team_engineering"). OE 2: Read comments on found NorthWind Linear issues using linear_list_comments to understand the full discussion history find details about the chain-of-custody protocol breach (movers arrived 2 hours early, loaded without the required IT security escort, servers sat overnight in a warehouse), Chloe's ops timeline reconstruction, and any engineering-specific follow-ups.
OE 3: Search Slack for NorthWind and chain-of-custody discussions using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "NorthWind" or "chain of custody" or "Raj Patel" or "Victor Huang" or "server relocation" or similar). OE 5: Search Airtable for the Raj Patel relocation record using airtable_search_records (query: "Raj Patel" or "NorthWind" or similar) to see what was actually documented in the system. OE 7: Look up Samira Tariq's contact details using contacts_search_contacts (query: "Samira Tariq" or "Samira" or similar). Find samira.tariq@moveops.com to use as the recipient for the email.

Note that David is assembling a retention response and engineering needs to be aware of what can and cannot be committed).

1 A Linear issue was created (via linear_create_issue) on team_engineering, OR a comment was added (via linear_create_comment) to an existing NorthWind or chain-of-custody-related issue.
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%

✓Run #2The agent called linear_create_issue with team 'Engineering' and the result confirms team_id 'team_engineering'. ENG-483 was successfully created.
✓Run #3The agent called linear_create_issue with team='team_engineering', creating ENG-483 titled 'Platform lacks chain-of-custody and handoff tracking for sensitive equipment moves'.

✓Run #5The agent called linear_create_issue with team='Engineering', and the result confirms team_id='team_engineering' and number=483.
✓Run #6The agent called linear_create_issue with team='Engineering', creating a new ticket titled 'Platform gap: No chain-of-custody tracking for sensitive equipment moves'. This satisfies the criterion of creating a Linear issue on team_engineering.
2 The Linear issue or comment (visible in linear_create_issue description or linear_create_comment body) describes a chain-of-custody or custody handoff tracking system limitation (e.g., 'no signed handoffs,' 'no audit timestamps,' 'custody logging gap' or similar).
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The Linear issue description explicitly states 'MoveOps has no platform-level support for chain-of-custody handoff logging. There is no mechanism to record signed handoffs, audit timestamps, handler identity verification, or custody transfer events.' This clearly describes the system limitation.
✓Run #2The Linear issue description explicitly states 'no signed digital handoffs, no audit timestamps, no tamper-evident custody logs, and no structured record of who handled what equipment at which transfer point,' directly describing the system limitation.
✓Run #3The Linear issue description explicitly lists: 'No signed handoff logs, no audit timestamps, no verification checkpoints, and no custody state tracking in our system.' All key tracking limitations are described.
✓Run #4The Linear issue description explicitly states 'MoveOps has no platform-level support for chain-of-custody tracking, signed handoff logging, or audit timestamping' and details that requirements 'lived in email threads and an Airtable text field' with 'no mechanism for recording escort sign-ins, logging handlers with timestamps, or generating auditable custody chain documents.'
✓Run #5The Linear issue description explicitly states 'no signed handoff logs, no audit timestamps for custody transfers, no digital verification gates for escort/supervision requirements, and no tamper-evident documentation trail.'
✓Run #6The Linear issue description explicitly states 'We currently have no signed handoff logging, no audit timestamps at transfer points, no verification checkpoints, and no delivery authorization controls' and references 'Every one of these moves is currently tracked with free-text notes in Airtable's Special Requirements field and ad-hoc Slack coordination.' This clearly describes the chain-of-custody system limitation.
3 The Linear issue or comment (visible in linear_create_issue description or linear_create_comment body) references the NorthWind Technologies Raj Patel server relocation as the triggering case.
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%

✓Run #3The Linear issue description explicitly references 'Case 1: NorthWind Technologies — Raj Patel server lab (Chicago → Denver, April 2026)' as the triggering incident.
✓Run #4The Linear issue description includes a section explicitly titled 'What happened — NorthWind Technologies (Raj Patel server lab move)' referencing the 4 rack-mounted servers with proprietary source code moving Chicago to Denver.
✓Run #5The Linear issue includes a dedicated section 'Triggering Incident: NorthWind / Raj Patel (April 2026)' and explicitly names the NorthWind server lab relocation as the triggering case.

8 The email to [samira.tariq@moveops.com](mailto:samira.tariq@moveops.com) (visible in send_email content parameter) describes the chain-of-custody tracking system limitation.
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The email content describes the technical system limitation: 'We have no platform-level chain-of-custody tracking. No signed handoff logging, no audit timestamps, no handler identity capture, no escort/authorization gates, no delivery recipient verification, no audit trail export.' It also includes a 7-point feature spec.
✓Run #2The email content includes a 'THE GAP:' section explicitly describing 'No digital handoff signatures, no audit timestamps, no tamper-evident custody logs,' clearly describing the system limitation in technical terms.
✓Run #3The email body states 'MoveOps has no platform-level support for chain-of-custody tracking. No signed handoff logs, no audit timestamps, no verification checkpoints that prevent a crew from proceeding without required authorization, no equipment custody state model' — clearly describing the system limitation in technical terms.
✓Run #4The email content includes a 'THE PROBLEM' section stating 'Our platform has no support for chain-of-custody tracking, signed handoff logging, or audit timestamping for equipment moves' and a 'WHAT WE NEED TO BUILD' section with structured field requirements.
✓Run #5The email explicitly describes the system limitation: 'MoveOps has no system-level support for chain-of-custody tracking on sensitive equipment moves. 10 The final response identifies that the NorthWind chain-of-custody breach involved movers arriving before the IT security escort, loading without supervised oversight, and/or servers being stored in a warehouse overnight.
outcome
 ✓✓✓✓✓✓ 100%
✓Run #1The final response states: 'SentinelChain's crew loaded 2 hours before the IT escort arrived and then warehoused the equipment overnight — both explicitly prohibited.' This covers movers arriving before escort and overnight warehousing.
✓Run #2The final response explicitly states 'crew loaded 2 hours early without the escort, equipment went to overnight warehouse storage,' identifying both the missing IT escort and overnight warehousing as specific breach details.
✓Run #3The final response states: 'The crew arrived at 7:00 AM and started loading before the 9:00 AM IT escort showed up, then placed the servers (containing proprietary source code) in overnight warehouse storage — both explicitly prohibited.'
✓Run #4The final response explicitly states: 'crew loaded 2 hours before the escort arrived, then put servers in overnight warehouse storage' — directly identifying both the missing IT escort and the unauthorized warehouse storage as the specific breach details.
✓Run #5The final response explicitly states: 'Crew arrived at 7:00 AM and loaded without waiting. Equipment went to overnight warehouse storage.' — covering both the early arrival without escort and the unauthorized warehouse storage.
✓Run #6The final response explicitly states 'the SentinelChain crew arrived 2 hours early (7 AM vs 9 AM escort), loaded without the IT escort, and placed servers in overnight warehouse storage — both explicitly prohibited.' All three specific breach details are present.
